hiking.name: Bondi to Coogee Coastal Walk
hiking.description: Stunning 6km coastal walk with ocean views, beaches, and rock pools along the way.

hiking.name: Manly to Spit Bridge Walk
hiking.description: 10km bushwalk through Sydney Harbour National Park with harbour views and Aboriginal rock engravings.

hiking.name: Blue Mountains National Park
hiking.description: World Heritage site with dramatic cliffs, eucalyptus forests, and the famous Three Sisters.

hiking.name: Barangaroo Reserve
hiking.description: Harbourfront park with native plants, walking paths, and stunning harbour views.

hiking.name: North Head Sanctuary
hiking.description: Former quarantine station with bushwalking trails and panoramic harbour views.

cafe_hopping.name: Bills Darlinghurst
cafe_hopping.description: Iconic Sydney cafe famous for ricotta hotcakes and scrambled eggs. A must-visit brunch spot.

cafe_hopping.name: Single O Surry Hills
cafe_hopping.description: Pioneer of Sydney's specialty coffee scene. Great pour-over and seasonal brunch menu.

cafe_hopping.name: Pablo & Rusty's
cafe_hopping.description: Student-friendly cafe near USyd campus with excellent coffee and affordable meals.

cafe_hopping.name: Cornersmith Marrickville
cafe_hopping.description: Sustainability-focused cafe with house-made pickles and seasonal local produce.

cafe_hopping.name: Reuben Hills
cafe_hopping.description: Trendy cafe in Surry Hills with Latin American inspired food and in-house roasted coffee.

art_exhibitions.name: Art Gallery of NSW
art_exhibitions.description: Major art museum with Australian, Aboriginal, European, and Asian art. Free general admission!

art_exhibitions.name: Museum of Contemporary Art
art_exhibitions.description: Free museum dedicated to contemporary art from Australia and around the world. Great harbour views.

art_exhibitions.name: White Rabbit Gallery
art_exhibitions.description: Free gallery of contemporary Chinese art. One of the most significant collections in the world.

art_exhibitions.name: Carriageworks
art_exhibitions.description: Contemporary multi-arts centre in a former railway workshop. Free weekend markets too!

art_exhibitions.name: Powerhouse Museum
art_exhibitions.description: Science, technology, design, and arts museum. Great for curious minds with interactive exhibits.

beaches.name: Bondi Beach
beaches.description: Australia's most famous beach. Great for swimming, surfing, and people-watching.

beaches.name: Manly Beach
beaches.description: Iconic Northern Beaches destination. Take the ferry from Circular Quay for amazing harbour views.

beaches.name: Coogee Beach
beaches.description: Family-friendly beach with a relaxed vibe, ocean pool, and great fish and chips nearby.

beaches.name: Bronte Beach
beaches.description: Beautiful sheltered beach with a natural rock pool and a lovely park for picnics.

beaches.name: Balmoral Beach
beaches.description: Calm harbour beach perfect for a relaxed swim. Great cafe scene and picnic spots.

budget_eats.name: Mamak (Haymarket)
budget_eats.description: Famous for authentic Malaysian roti and satay. Always busy, always worth the wait. Budget-friendly!

budget_eats.name: Chat Thai
budget_eats.description: Authentic Thai street food at student-friendly prices. Try the pad thai and mango sticky rice!

budget_eats.name: Din Tai Fung (World Square)
budget_eats.description: World-famous xiao long bao (soup dumplings). Watch chefs make them through the glass!

budget_eats.name: El Jannah (Granville)
budget_eats.description: Legendary Lebanese charcoal chicken with the best garlic sauce in Sydney. Huge portions, small price.

budget_eats.name: Spice Alley (Chippendale)
budget_eats.description: Hawker-style food court with Thai, Malaysian, Vietnamese, and Japanese stalls. Cheap and delicious!

budget_eats.name: Paddy's Markets
budget_eats.description: Historic market with cheap Asian food, fresh produce, and bargain shopping. Student paradise!
